name: Remote Transmitter → Transmit Abbwelcome
description: This [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions) sends a ABB-Welcome message to the intercom bus. The message type, addresses, address length and data can vary a lot between ABB-Welcome systems. Please refer to the received messages while performing actions like ringing a doorbell or opening a door.

config_entries.label: Destination Address
config_entries.description: The destination address to send the command to, see received messages for more info.

config_entries.label: Message Type
config_entries.description: The message type, see dumper output for more info. The highest bit indicates a reply.

config_entries.label: Source Address
config_entries.description: The source address to send the command from, see received messages for more info. For indoor stations the last byte of the address represents the apartment number set by the dials on the back of the indoor station and is transmitted in hexadecimal format.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The code to send. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output. Defaults to `[]`

config_entries.label: Message ID
config_entries.description: The message ID, see dumper output for more info. Defaults to a randomly generated ID if this message is not a reply or retransmission.

config_entries.label: Retransmission
config_entries.description: Should only be `true` if this message has been transmitted before with the same `message_id`. Typically, messages are transmitted up to three times with a 1 second interval if no reply is received. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Three Byte Address
config_entries.description: The length of the source and destination address. `false` means two bytes and `true` means three bytes. Please check the received messages to see which address length is used by your system. For example, `[XXXX > XXXX]` appears in the receiver log for two byte addresses and `[XXXXXX > XXXXXX]` for three byte addresses. Defaults to `false`.
